riskTitle: Continued Load-Shedding and Generation Capacity Shortfall
description: Eskom's aging coal fleet and persistent plant breakdowns result in load-shedding reaching Stage 6 and above, causing severe economic damage estimated at R1 billion per day. Unplanned outages exceed 15,000 MW, far above the 4,000 MW target.
mitigationPlan: Accelerate maintenance programme, procure emergency power from IPPs, fast-track Kusile and Medupi completion, implement demand-side management.

riskTitle: Debt Sustainability and Sovereign Guarantee Exposure
description: Eskom's total debt exceeds R400 billion, of which approximately R350 billion is government-guaranteed. The debt service obligations consume a significant portion of revenue, threatening financial viability and placing pressure on the national fiscus.
mitigationPlan: Implement R254 billion government debt relief package, improve revenue collection, reduce operational costs, pursue tariff increases through NERSA.

riskTitle: Just Energy Transition and Stranded Asset Risk
description: South Africa's commitment to the Just Energy Transition Partnership (JETP) requires phasing out coal-fired power stations. Eskom faces risk of stranded assets worth hundreds of billions of rands if transition is not managed carefully.
mitigationPlan: Develop Just Energy Transition plan, engage with JETP partners for funding, plan for repurposing or decommissioning of coal assets, invest in renewable energy.

riskTitle: Procurement Irregularities and State Capture Legacy
description: Eskom was a primary target of State Capture as documented by the Zondo Commission. Legacy irregular expenditure exceeds R50 billion. Ongoing SIU investigations and civil recovery actions create reputational and financial risk.
mitigationPlan: Implement Zondo Commission recommendations, strengthen procurement controls, pursue civil recovery, cooperate with SIU and NPA investigations.

description: Widespread theft of copper cables, signalling equipment and rail infrastructure causes significant operational disruptions. Cable theft incidents exceed 1,000 per month, costing Transnet over R5 billion annually and severely impacting freight volumes.
mitigationPlan: Deploy private security, install fibre optic cables to replace copper, engage SAPS and communities, implement anti-theft technology, establish rapid response teams.

riskTitle: Port Congestion at Durban and Cape Town
description: Chronic congestion at the Port of Durban, South Africa's busiest port, results in vessel waiting times exceeding 10 days. Container throughput is significantly below capacity, costing the economy an estimated R1.5 billion per month.
mitigationPlan: Implement Operation Phakisa port efficiency programme, procure additional port equipment, introduce private terminal operators, extend operating hours.

description: Transnet's total debt exceeds R130 billion. Declining freight volumes due to rail infrastructure failures reduce revenue, while debt service costs remain high. The company faces risk of covenant breaches on existing debt facilities.
mitigationPlan: Negotiate debt restructuring with lenders, improve freight volumes through infrastructure investment, pursue private sector participation to reduce capital requirements.

description: Following the July 2021 ransomware attack that paralysed Transnet's port operations for several days, the company remains vulnerable to sophisticated cyber threats. Critical operational technology systems are inadequately protected.
mitigationPlan: Implement comprehensive cybersecurity framework, upgrade OT/IT systems, conduct regular penetration testing, establish 24/7 security operations centre.

description: SAA faces intense competition from low-cost carriers (FlySafair, Lift, Airlink) on domestic routes and from Ethiopian Airlines, Kenya Airways and Gulf carriers on international routes. Market share has declined significantly post-business rescue.
mitigationPlan: Develop competitive pricing strategy, focus on profitable routes, leverage Takatso partnership for network expansion, improve customer experience and loyalty programme.

description: The 51% equity sale to the Takatso Consortium has faced regulatory and commercial delays. Failure to complete the transaction would leave SAA without the private sector capital and expertise needed for sustainable operations.
mitigationPlan: Engage with Takatso Consortium to resolve outstanding conditions precedent, obtain necessary regulatory approvals, maintain government support during transition.

companyName: Passenger Rail Agency of South Africa
riskTitle: Rolling Stock Failure and Service Cancellations
description: PRASA's aging rolling stock fleet has a high failure rate, resulting in widespread service cancellations. Only 40% of scheduled services operate reliably. Commuters are forced to use expensive minibus taxis, increasing transport costs for the poor.
mitigationPlan: Accelerate Rolling Stock Fleet Renewal Programme, implement emergency maintenance programme, prioritise high-density corridors.

companyName: Passenger Rail Agency of South Africa
description: PRASA has accumulated billions of rands in irregular expenditure. The Auditor-General has issued adverse audit opinions for multiple consecutive years. Irregular expenditure includes the controversial R51 billion Rolling Stock Fleet Renewal Programme.
mitigationPlan: Implement consequence management, strengthen supply chain management, appoint qualified CFO, implement recommendations from Auditor-General.

description: Denel has been technically insolvent, unable to pay salaries and supplier invoices. Government bailouts totalling R3.4 billion have provided temporary relief but the underlying business model remains unviable without significant restructuring.
mitigationPlan: Implement government recapitalisation, restructure operations, focus on profitable product lines, pursue export contracts, consider strategic partnerships.

riskTitle: Loss of Key Technical Skills and Intellectual Property
description: Denel has lost hundreds of critical engineers and technical specialists due to non-payment of salaries and poor working conditions. This threatens the company's ability to deliver on existing contracts and maintain its defence technology capabilities.
mitigationPlan: Implement competitive retention packages, regularise salary payments, create career development pathways, partner with universities for skills pipeline.

companyName: South African Broadcasting Corporation SOC Limited
riskTitle: TV Licence Revenue Decline and Collection Failure
description: TV licence compliance rate has fallen below 30%, with millions of South Africans not paying the annual R225 licence fee. The SABC loses over R1 billion annually in uncollected licence fees, threatening its public service mandate.
mitigationPlan: Pursue legislative reform to modernise TV licence system, improve collection mechanisms, explore alternative funding models including government appropriation.

companyName: Airports Company South Africa SOC Limited
description: ACSA must maintain compliance with ICAO standards and SACAA regulations. Any safety or security incidents at managed airports could result in loss of international certifications, severely impacting passenger volumes and revenue.
mitigationPlan: Maintain rigorous safety management systems, conduct regular audits, invest in security technology, train staff to international standards.

companyName: Airports Company South Africa SOC Limited
description: ACSA's aeronautical charges are regulated by SACAA. Regulatory decisions that limit tariff increases below inflation could constrain ACSA's ability to fund capital expenditure for airport expansion and maintenance.
mitigationPlan: Engage proactively with SACAA on tariff reviews, diversify revenue through non-aeronautical commercial activities, improve cost efficiency.
